description: Enrichment Foundation — Provider registry, geographic/economic data, vertical configuration pack

tasks.title: Vertical Configuration Pack — Dental default
tasks.prompt:
Create the first Vertical Configuration Pack (M7.9) as a well-structured JSON configuration that the intelligence service loads.

Create src/Snapp.Service.Intelligence/Config/verticals/dental.json containing:

1. Scoring dimensions: 6 dimensions with names, weights, thresholds (strong/acceptable/weak), KPI definitions per dimension
2. Contribution categories: each with field definitions (type, label, validation rules, confidence weight). Categories: Revenue, StaffCount, Utilization, ClientVolume, RevenueMix, OwnerProduction, EquipmentAge, FacilityType
3. Career stage rules: signals mapped to each of 6 stages, risk flag triggers
4. Benchmark metrics: list of metrics computed at each geographic level
5. Valuation drivers: list with impact weights
6. Registry source: NPI/NPPES configuration (taxonomy codes for dental: 122300000X etc.)
7. Role definitions: staff roles relevant to this vertical (hygienist, assistant, associate, front desk, office manager)

Also create src/Snapp.Service.Intelligence/Config/verticals/schema.json — JSON Schema for the vertical config format, so other verticals can be validated.

Create a Snapp.Shared/Configuration/VerticalConfig.cs model class that deserializes the JSON config. Add loader to ServiceCollectionExtensions.

Unit tests: verify dental config loads and validates. Verify schema validation catches missing required fields.
tasks.done_when: Dental vertical config JSON exists. Schema validates it. C# model deserializes it. Unit tests pass.

tasks.title: Provider Registry Enrichment Service
tasks.prompt:
Implement the Provider/Practitioner Registry module (M7.1) as a scheduled enrichment job.

Create Snapp.Service.Enrichment as a scheduled job (console app) that:

1. Reads the active vertical config to determine which registry to query
2. For the dental vertical: queries NPPES API (or loads from NPPES data files) for dental providers by taxonomy code
3. For each provider: extracts identity, specialty, practice address, enumeration date, co-located provider count
4. Stores as SIGNAL# items in snapp-intel table with confidence scores
5. Updates user profiles with enrichment data where email/NPI can be matched

For local dev/testing: create a fixture of 50-100 synthetic provider records that the enrichment job can load from a local JSON file instead of hitting the real NPPES API. The job should support both modes (config: Enrichment:Source = 'fixture' | 'api').

Also implement Geographic & Economic Data (M7.5): load county-level fixture data (population, median income, provider density) for 10-20 test counties. Store as MKT# items.

Dockerfile + docker-compose entry (snapp-enrichment). Can be run manually: `docker compose run snapp-enrichment`.

Integration tests: run enrichment with fixture data, verify SIGNAL# and MKT# items created in DynamoDB.
tasks.done_when: Enrichment job loads fixture data into snapp-intel. Provider signals and market profiles created. Tests pass.

tasks.title: Sprint 8 integration validation
tasks.prompt: Validate Sprint 8. Run enrichment job, verify data in DynamoDB, verify intelligence dashboard now shows enriched data (market profiles, provider signals). Run all tests. Write .claude/reports/sprint-8-validation.md.
tasks.done_when: Enrichment data populates intelligence layer. All tests pass. Validation report written.
